note:
英特尔报告称，第二季度收入增长了20%，达到197亿美元，因为大流行促使人们购买个人电脑，这样他们就可以更好地在家工作。但该公司表示，在扩大其7纳米制造工厂的过程中遇到了延误，这导致该公司的股票在盘后交易中下跌。
与一年前相比，以PC为中心的收入增长了7%，而以数据为中心的收入增长了34%，占总收入的52%。每股收益为1.19美元，增长29%，非GAAP每股收益为1.23美元。分析师此前预计，英特尔公布的每股收益为1.11美元，营收为185.4亿美元。在盘后交易中，英特尔股价下跌9%，至每股54.93美元。
英特尔首席执行官鲍勃·斯旺(Bob Swan)在一份声明中表示，这是一个“非常好的季度，远远超出了我们的预期”。他将这一表现归功于云交付服务、在家工作和学习的环境，以及5G网络的建设。
英特尔表示，它正在加快向10纳米的过渡，但它指出，与之前的预期相比，其下一代制造-将提供更高的产量、更快的芯片和更低的成本-已经推迟。
英特尔预计，全年非GAAP每股收益为4.85美元，营收为750亿美元。第二季度，数据中心集团的收入为71亿美元，增长了43%。物联网集团营收为6.7亿美元，下滑32%。Mobileye的收入为1.46亿美元，下降了27%。NSG(闪存)营收为17亿美元，增长76%。PSG(可编程芯片)营收为5.01亿美元，增长2%。个人电脑部门CCG的收入为95亿美元，增长了7%。
英特尔表示，云、笔记本、内存和5G产品的销售强劲。英特尔在本季度推出了第三代英特尔至强可扩展处理器。
英特尔表示，由于新冠肺炎的持续工作和在家学习的动态推动笔记本电脑走强，本季度个人电脑芯片销售强劲，随着需求转向笔记本电脑，这也是导致台式机销量下降的原因之一。
英特尔即将推出代号为老虎湖(Tiger Lake)的10纳米产品，并准备在今年年底推出冰湖服务器中央处理器(CPU)。
在2021年下半年，英特尔预计将交付新的客户端CPU系列(代号为Alder Lake)，其中将包括其首个基于10 nm的台式CPU，以及新的基于10 nm的服务器CPU(代号为Sapphire Rapids)。
与之前的预期相比，该公司基于7纳米的CPU产品的推出时间大约有6个月的变化。主要的驱动因素是英特尔7纳米制程的成品率，根据最近的数据，这一成品率现在比公司的内部目标晚了一年。
采用7纳米制程，电路之间的宽度是7纳米宽，其中纳米是十亿分之一米。通过从10纳米到7纳米的制造，英特尔可以使其电路小型化，降低成本，加快性能，并加快生产。
Moor Insights&Amp；Strategy分析师帕特里克·穆尔黑德(Patrick Moorhead)在给VentureBeat的一封电子邮件中表示：“7 nm的(延迟)并不是一个积极的声明，因为许多产品都依赖于它。”了解英特尔，它的备份总是有备份的，我相信我们会听到关于增强10纳米以提高其竞争力的消息。该公司在财务上做得很好，特别是在数据中心、笔记本电脑和商业产品线上，当时世界其他地区都在使用台积电的10纳米技术。“
note_en:
Intel reported that it grew revenues 20% to $19.7 billion in the second quarter as the pandemic spurred people to buy PCs so they cold work from home better. But the company said it ran into delays in ramping up its 7-nanometer manufacturing plants, which sent the company’s stock downward in after-hours trading.
 PC-centric revenue grew 7% compared to a year ago, while data-centric revenue grew 34%, accounting for 52% of overall revenue. Earnings per share was $1.19, up 29%, while non-GAAP earnings per share was $1.23. Analysts had expected Intel to report earnings of $1.11 a share on revenue for $18.54 billion. In after-hours trading, Intel’s shares are down 9% to $54.93 a share.
  Intel CEO Bob Swan said it was an “excellent quarter, well above our expectations” in a statement. He credited the performance to cloud-delivered services, a work- and learn-at-home environment, and the buildout of 5G networks.
 Intel said that it is accelerating its transition to 10-nanometer, but it noted that its next-generation manufacturing — which would deliver higher production, faster chips, and lower costs — has been delayed compared to its prior expectations.
 For the full year, Intel expects non-GAAP earnings per share of $4.85 on revenue of $75 billion. For the second quarter, the datacenter group had revenue of $7.1 billion, up 43%. The internet of things group had revenue of $670 million, down 32%. Mobileye revenue was $146 million, down 27%. NSG (flash memory) revenue was $1.7 billion, up 76%. PSG (programmable chips) revenue was $501 million, up 2%. And the PC division, CCG, saw revenue of $9.5 billion, up 7%.
 Intel said it saw strong sales of cloud, notebook, memory and 5G products. Intel introduced its third-generation Intel Xeon Scalable processors during the quarter.
  Intel said PC chip sales were strong in the quarter thanks to notebook strength driven by the continued work- and learn at home dynamics of COVID-19, which also contributed to a volume decline in desktops as demand shifted to laptops.
 Intel has its code-named Tiger Lake 10-nanometer products launching soon, and it is prepping Ice Lake server central processing units (CPUs) for launch by the end of the year.
 In the second half of 2021, Intel expects to deliver a new line of client CPU’s (code-named Alder Lake), which will include its first 10nm-based desktop CPU, and a new 10nm-based server CPU (code-named Sapphire Rapids).
 The company’s 7-nm-based CPU product timing is shifting approximately six months relative to prior expectations. The primary driver is the yield of Intel’s 7-nm process, which based on recent data, is now trending a year behind the company’s internal targets.
 With a 7-nm process, the width between circuits is 7 nanometers wide, where a nanometer is a billionth of a meter. By moving from 10-nm to 7-nm manufacturing, Intel can miniaturize its circuits, cut costs, speed up the performance, and speed up production.
 “The 7-nm [delay] isn’t a positive announcement as many products were dependent on it,” said Patrick Moorhead, analyst at Moor Insights & Strategy, in an email to VentureBeat. “Knowing Intel, it always has backups for its backups and I am sure we will be hearing about enhancements to 10-nm to increase its competitiveness. The company has done well financially, particularly well in the datacenter, notebook and commercial product lines, on 14-nm when the rest of the world was on TSMC’s 10-nm.”
title: 英特尔向7纳米移动延迟

note:
英特尔股价周四下跌多达9%，此前该公司公布的第二季获利好于预期，并表示下一代芯片的问世时间将晚于预期。
根据Refinitiv的数据，调整后每股收益为1.23美元，而分析师预期为每股1.11美元。
在指引方面，英特尔预计第三财季调整后每股收益为1.10美元，营收为182亿美元。接受Refinitiv调查的分析师此前预计，调整后每股收益为1.14美元，营收为179亿美元。
一个季度前，英特尔选择不给出全年业绩指引，但在周四，英特尔宣布了对全年的新预测。该公司现在预计调整后每股收益为4.85美元，营收为750亿美元。接受Refinitiv调查的分析师的共识是调整后每股收益为4.81美元，营收为738.6亿美元。
该公司宣布进一步推迟生产带有微型7纳米晶体管的芯片，以提高性能，以求赶上AMD，后者已经有7纳米产品可用。英特尔在一份声明中表示，主要驱动因素是英特尔7 nm制程的成品率，根据最近的数据，这一成品率目前的趋势比该公司的内部目标晚了大约12个月。英特尔发布声明数小时后，AMD股价上涨6%。
在推出有竞争力的芯片之前，英特尔遇到了供应问题，这反过来又影响了戴尔和惠普等设备制造商。
英特尔说，在截至6月27日的季度里，英特尔的收入同比增长了20%，而上一季度的增幅为23%。
英特尔最大的业务部门--为个人电脑生产芯片的客户计算集团(Client Computing Group)在本季度实现了95亿美元的收入，按年率计算增长了7%，超过了FactSet普遍预期的91亿美元。行业研究集团高德纳(Gartner)估计，在第一季度因疫情而下降后，第二季度个人电脑出货量恢复了同比增长。
专注于云提供商和服务器制造商芯片的数据中心集团贡献了71.2亿美元的收入，增长了43%，高于66.1亿美元的FactSet共识。
非易失性存储器解决方案部门的收入为16.6亿美元，增长了76%，高于市场普遍预期的12.9亿美元。
在本季度，英特尔宣布以9亿美元收购移动初创公司Moovit，并以1.5亿美元将其家庭网关平台业务出售给美国MaxLine。该公司还推出了用于游戏PC和笔记本电脑的新芯片。
剔除盘后走势，英特尔股价自年初以来已上涨约1%，而标准普尔500指数持平。
高管们将在下午5点的电话会议上与分析师讨论结果。东部时间。
更正：本文最初包括英特尔的季度每股收益，而不是调整后的每股收益。
note_en:
Intel shares fell as much as 9% on Thursday after the company reported better-than-expected second-quarter earnings and said next-generation chips would come out later than it had expected.
  Earnings: $1.23 per share, adjusted, vs. $1.11 per share as expected by analysts, according to Refinitiv.
  With respect to guidance, Intel called for $1.10 in earnings per share on an adjusted basis and $18.2 billion in revenue for its fiscal third quarter. Analysts polled by Refinitiv had expected $1.14 in adjusted earnings per share on $17.90 billion in revenue.
 One quarter ago Intel chose not to give full-year guidance, but on Thursday it announced a new forecast for the full year. It now sees $4.85 in adjusted earnings per share and $75 billion in revenue. The consensus among analysts polled by Refinitiv was $4.81 in adjusted earnings per share and revenue of $73.86 billion.
 The company announced further delays to production of chips with tiny 7-nanometer transistors for higher performance as it seeks to catch up with  AMD, which already has 7-nanometer products available. "The primary driver is the yield of Intel's 7nm process, which based on recent data, is now trending approximately twelve months behind the company's internal target," the company said in a  statement. AMD stock rose 6% after hours following Intel announcement.
 The struggle to bring out competitive chips comes after Intel ran into  supply issues, which in turn affected device makers such as  Dell and  HP.
 Intel's revenue increased 20% year over year for the quarter that ended on June 27, compared with 23% growth  one quarter earlier, Intel said.
  Intel's top business unit, the Client Computing Group that makes chips for PCs, delivered $9.50 billion in revenue in the quarter, growing 7% on an annualized basis and topping the FactSet consensus estimate of $9.10 billion. Industry research group  Gartner estimated that second-quarter PC shipments returned to year-over-year growth in the quarter, after a decline in the first quarter in connection with the pandemic.
 The Data Center Group, which focuses on chips for cloud providers and server makers, contributed $7.12 billion in revenue, up 43% and above the $6.61 billion FactSet consensus.
 The Non-Volatile Memory Solutions Group had $1.66 billion in revenue, representing 76% growth and coming in higher than the $1.29 billion consensus.
 In the quarter Intel announced the $900 million acquisition of mobility start-up  Moovit and the $150 million sale of its home gateway platform business to  MaxLinear. It also introduced new chips for  gaming PCs and  laptops.
 Leaving out the after-hours move, Intel shares have risen about 1% since the start of the year, while the S&P 500 is flat.
 Executives will discuss the results with analysts on a conference call at 5 p.m. Eastern time.
  Correction: This article originally included Intel's quarterly earnings per share rather than adjusted earnings per share.

title: 安全建议和JSA-2020-0001

note:
人权联盟说，新疆地区营地生产的棉花进入了全球五分之一的棉花产品。
一个由180多个人权组织组成的联盟表示，世界上许多最大的时尚品牌和零售商参与了中国西北新疆地区数百万维吾尔族人遭受的强迫劳动和侵犯人权的行为。
针对该地区维吾尔族人口的暴行，包括酷刑、强迫分离和对维吾尔族妇女强制绝育，全球的愤怒情绪日益高涨。
人权组织联盟表示，尽管存在这些虐待行为，但世界上许多领先的服装品牌仍在采购棉花和纱线，这些棉花和纱线是通过一个庞大的国家支持的强迫劳动体系生产的，该体系涉及新疆战俘营、工厂和农场中的多达180万维吾尔族和其他突厥族和穆斯林人。它说，整个地区的强迫劳动制度是二战以来对少数民族和宗教少数群体的最大拘留。
全球时尚品牌如此广泛地从新疆采购，以至于该联盟估计，“几乎可以肯定”，全球销售的多达五分之一的棉制品受到那里发生的强迫劳动和侵犯人权行为的污染。
中国是世界上最大的棉花生产国，84%的棉花来自新疆地区。新疆生产的棉花和纱线在孟加拉国、柬埔寨和越南等其他主要服装生产国得到广泛使用。新疆的棉花和纱线也用于纺织品和家居。本周，“纽约时报”报道说，该地区的工厂也在向世界各国供应口罩和其他个人防护用品。
该联盟公布了一份广泛的品牌清单，声称这些品牌继续从该地区或与维吾尔族人强迫劳动有关的工厂采购，包括Gap、C&amp；A、阿迪达斯、无印良品、汤米·希尔费格(Tommy Hilfiger)和卡尔文·克莱恩(Calvin Klein)。
该联盟在今天发布的一份声明中表示：“实际上，整个(全球)服装行业都被强迫的维吾尔族和突厥语穆斯林劳工玷污了。”
该联盟表示，更多的领先服装品牌也继续与中国企业保持有利可图的战略合作伙伴关系，接受政府补贴，以扩大该地区的纺织品生产，或者受益于从新疆转移到中国各地工厂的维吾尔族人的强迫劳动。
反奴隶制国际组织(Anti-Slavery International)商业和人权经理克洛伊·克兰斯顿(Chloe Cranston)表示：“每个商业街和奢侈品牌都很有可能与维吾尔族人的遭遇有关。”
该联盟包括70多个维吾尔人权组织、反奴隶制组织和劳工维权人士。该联盟在行动呼吁中表示，全球服装业必须在一年内根除所有与新疆强迫劳动有关的产品和材料。
“全球品牌需要问问自己，他们对针对维吾尔族人的种族灭绝政策做出了多大的贡献。这些公司不知何故设法避免了对这一政策中的共谋的审查--这一点今天停止了，“维吾尔人权项目执行主任奥马尔·卡纳特(Omer Kanat)说。
根据呼吁采取行动的签字人之一工人权利联盟(WRC)的说法，各品牌没有可信的方法来证明他们来自新疆的供应链没有强迫劳动。
“如果维吾尔地区的强迫劳工说出实情，他们将面临恶毒的报复。这使得通过劳工检查进行尽职调查变得不可能，而且几乎保证了任何从维吾尔地区采购的品牌都在使用强迫劳动，“WRC执行董事斯科特·诺瓦(Scott Nova)表示。
“如果一家服装品牌声称有信心地知道它在该地区使用的所有农场和工厂都没有强迫劳动，那要么非常愤世嫉俗，要么就是信息错误。”
今年4月，由人权律师组成的全球法律行动网络(GRAN)也向HMRC提供了证据，证明无印良品(Muji)、优衣库(Uniqlo)、H&amp；M和宜家(Ikea)等品牌在英国销售含有新疆地区棉花和纱线的产品。格兰辩称，英国政府应该停止在整个地区销售与强迫劳动有关的产品，因为这违反了几项英国法律，包括1897年“外国制造商品法”(Foreign Made Goods Act)。
对此，H&amp；M和宜家表示，他们将停止从该地区购买棉花。在提交给“卫报”的一份最新声明中，H&amp；M表示，它与在该地区运营的一家纱线生产商有间接关系，但表示正在评估这种关系。
无印良品证实继续使用来自新疆的棉纱，但否认其棉花和纱线与强迫劳动有关。“我们的商业伙伴(向我们保证)生产我们产品的人有良好的工作条件，受到尊重，独立审计师对这些棉纺厂进行了现场审计，并确认没有证据表明他们的设施存在强迫劳动和少数民族宗教歧视的情况。
优衣库的一位发言人表示，优衣库在该地区没有生产任何产品，并坚称其供应链中的所有生产合作伙伴都必须遵守他们关于人权和工人权利的行为准则。
在一份声明中，拥有卡尔文·克莱恩(Calvin Klein)和汤米·希尔费格(Tommy Hilfiger)的PVH Corporation表示，它不会从该地区采购成品服装，并将在未来12个月内停止与新疆任何生产服装或面料或使用种植的棉花的工厂和工厂的所有业务关系。
阿迪达斯表示，它不从新疆采购货物，并已指示供应商不要从该地区采购纱线。
C&amp；A的一位发言人表示，该公司没有从任何制造商那里采购，也没有与该地区的任何面料或纱厂合作。
然而，联盟成员表示，品牌和零售商仅仅切断与供应商的直接关系是不够的，必须对该行业与该地区的联系进行彻底改革。
克兰斯顿说：“这不仅关系到直接的供应链联系，还关系到全球服装行业如何帮助支撑和促进侵犯人权和强迫劳动的体系。”“需要对品牌和零售商与维吾尔族人正在发生的大规模事件之间的联系进行深入而彻底的盘问。”
中国在新疆的人权记录招致了越来越多的国际谴责。本月早些时候，美国对中国官员实施制裁，以抗议维吾尔人和包括哈萨克人在内的其他少数民族的待遇。
上周，中国驻英国大使否认他的政府侵犯人权，此前网上再次出现的视频似乎显示，戴着镣铐、蒙着眼睛的维吾尔族囚犯在新疆被装上火车。
note_en:
Human rights coalition says cotton produced in camps in Xinjiang region finds its way into one in five cotton products worldwide
    Many of the world’s biggest fashion brands and retailers are complicit in the forced labour and human rights violations being perpetrated on millions of Uighur people in the Xinjiang region of northwestern China, says a coalition of more than 180 human rights groups.
 There is mounting global outrage over the atrocities being committed against the Uighur population in the region, including torture, forced separation and the  compulsory sterilisation of Uighur women.
  Despite these abuses, the  coalition of human rights groups says many of the world’s leading clothing brands continue to source cotton and yarn produced through a vast state-sponsored system of forced labour involving  up to 1.8m Uighur   and other Turkic and Muslim people in prison camps, factories and farms in Xinjiang. It says that the forced labour system across the region is the largest internment of an ethnic and religious minority since the second world war.
  Global fashion brands source so extensively from Xinjiang that the coalition estimates it is “virtually certain” that as many as one in five cotton products sold across the world are tainted with forced labour and human rights violations occurring there.
 China is the largest cotton producer in the world, with 84% of its cotton coming from the Xinjiang region. Cotton and yarn produced in Xinjiang are used extensively in other key garment-producing countries such as Bangladesh, Cambodia and Vietnam. Xinjiang cotton and yarn are also used in textiles and home furnishings. This week the New York Times  reported that factories in the region were also supplying face masks and other PPE to countries around the world.
 The coalition has  published an extensive list of brands it claims continue to source from the region, or from factories connected to the forced labour of Uighur people, including Gap, C&A, Adidas, Muji, Tommy Hilfiger and Calvin Klein.
  “Virtually the entire [global] apparels industry is tainted by forced Uighur and Turkic Muslim labour,” the coalition said in a statement issued today.
 The coalition says many more leading clothing brands also continue to maintain lucrative strategic partnerships with Chinese companies, accepting subsidies from their government to expand textile production in the region or benefiting from the forced labour of Uighur people transferred from Xinjiang to factories across China.
 “There is a high likelihood that every high street and luxury brand runs the risk of being linked to what is happening to the Uighur people,” says Chloe Cranston, business and human rights manager at Anti-Slavery International.
 In a call to action, the coalition, which includes more than 70 Uighur rights groups, anti-slavery organisations and labour rights campaigners, says the global apparel industry must eradicate all products and materials linked to forced labour in Xinjiang within a year.
 “Global brands need to ask themselves how comfortable they are contributing to a genocidal policy against the Uighur people. These companies have somehow managed to avoid scrutiny for complicity in that very policy – this stops today,” said Omer Kanat, executive director of the  Uyghur Human Rights Project.
 According to the Worker Rights Consortium (WRC), one of the signatories of the call to action, brands have no credible way of proving that their supply chains from the Xinjiang are free of forced labour.
 “Forced labourers in the Uighur region face vicious retaliation if they tell the truth about their circumstances. This makes due diligence through labour inspections impossible and virtually guarantees that any brand sourcing from the Uighur region is using forced labour,” said Scott Nova, executive director of the WRC.
 “An apparel brand that claims to know, with confidence, that all the farms and factories it uses in the region are free of forced labour is either deeply cynical or misinformed.”
 In April, the Global Legal Action Network (Glan), a group of human rights lawyers, also  provided evidence to  HMRC that brands including Muji, Uniqlo, H&M and Ikea were selling products in the UK containing cotton and yarn from the Xinjiang  region. Glan argued that the UK government should halt sales of products linked to forced labour across the region as it breached several UK laws including the 1897  Foreign Made Goods Act.
 In response, H&M and Ikea  said they would stop buying cotton from the  region. In an updated statement to The Guardian, H&M said that it had an indirect relationship with one yarn producer operating in the region but said it was reviewing the relationship.
  Muji confirmed that it continues to use cotton yarn from Xinjiang but denies that its cotton and yarn are connected to forced labour. “Our business partner [assures] us that the people who make our products have good working conditions and are treated with respect, the independent auditors have conducted on-site audit on these cotton spinning mills and have confirmed that there is no evidence of forced labour and discrimination of ethnoreligious minorities at their facilities.
 A Uniqlo spokesperson said that no Uniqlo product is manufactured in the region and insists that all production partners in its supply chain uphold their codes of conduct on human and workers rights.
 In a statement, PVH Corporation, which owns Calvin Klein and Tommy Hilfiger, said it did not source finished garments from the region and would cease all business relationships with any factories and mills that produce garments or fabric, or use cotton grown, in Xinjiang within the next 12 months.
 Adidas said it does not source goods from Xinjiang and have instructed its suppliers not to source yarn from the region.
 A C&A spokesperson said it did not source from any manufacturers or work with any fabric or yarn mills in the region.
 Yet members of the coalition said that it was not sufficient for brands and retailers to just sever direct relationships to suppliers but that a complete overhaul of the sector’s links to the region had to be undertaken.
 “This isn’t just about direct supply chain links, it’s about how the global apparels sector is helping prop up and facilitate the system of human rights abuses and forced labour,” says Cranston. “There needs to be a deep and thorough interrogation of how brands and retailers are linked to what is happening at scale to the Uighur people.”
  China’s human rights record in Xinjiang has provoked growing international condemnation. Earlier this month, the US imposed sanctions on Chinese officials in protest at the treatment of the Uighur and other minority groups, including Kazakhs.
 Last week the Chinese ambassador to the UK  denied his government was committing human rights violations  after videos resurfaced online appearing to show shackled and blindfolded Uighur prisoners being loaded on to trains in Xinjiang.
title: “几乎整个”时尚业与维吾尔族强迫劳动同谋
title_en: 'Virtually entire' fashion industry complicit in Uighur forced labour

note:
新公布的电子邮件显示，司法部的一名代表在2018年建议，联邦调查局(FBI)应该调查美国国家公共广播电台(NPR&#39；)使用安全、加密的回线的情况。
记者杰森·利奥波德(Jason Leopold)根据信息自由法(FOIA)的要求获得了美国司法部官员的电子邮件往来，并在Twitter上分享了这些电子邮件。这封电子邮件以尼尔·麦凯布(Neil McCabe)2018年4月的一条消息开始，麦凯布当时是One America News Network(OANN)的记者，OANN是一个以宣传和传播阴谋论而闻名的极右翼有线新闻频道。麦凯布写信给当时在司法部公共事务办公室(即司法部的沟通和公关部)担任发言人的劳伦·埃萨姆·戈里(Lauren Ehrsam Gorey)。
你能知道美国司法部对美国国家公共广播电台(NPR)发送一封支持Tor的TIP电子邮件是否介意吗？&#34；麦凯布写道，并添加了一个链接，指向美国国家公共广播电台(NPR)发送机密小费的说明。
美国国家公共广播电台(National Public Radio)是一家独立的非营利性广播和媒体公司，在全国拥有约1000家成员电台，这与ABC或CBS等电视网在大多数城市都有当地附属电台的方式非常相似。然而，麦凯布对向美国司法部询问NPR‘s Tipline的解释似乎表明，他认为这是一个国营渠道：
在陆军公共事务中，公共事务士兵之所以不会爆料犯罪或腐败的故事，是因为陆军实际上有自己的警察和侦探，即国会议员。这使得陆军报纸的一些记者追踪犯罪行为变得荒谬-相反，一旦公共事务士兵意识到不当行为，他就应该向当局举报。
那么，联邦政府雇员为什么会运行自己的私人网站，以获取有关犯罪或国家安全风险的信息？
然而，司法部代表伊恩·普赖尔(Ian Prior)并没有以任何方式纠正麦凯布，而是回应说：你应该为此联系联邦调查局。
正在讨论的支持Tor的TIP电子邮件是SecureDrop，这是一个由自由新闻基金会管理的开源文档提交系统。NPR使用SecureDrop允许个人发送匿名提示。大多数主要的国内和国际媒体，包括美联社、纽约时报、华盛顿邮报、ProPublica、路透社和其他几十家媒体也使用SecureDrop。
任何给定的新闻编辑部的SecureDrop门户网站都只能通过Tor浏览器访问，这是一种匿名软件，它积极地阻止使大多数网络用户容易识别的跟踪。
因为Tor让识别用户变得更加困难，所以它经常被用作通往黑暗网络的门户，也就是互联网上犯罪和犯罪分子往往聚集的那个看不见的角落。但是，有人想要屏蔽自己的身份有很多正当的法律原因-包括但不限于，与调查记者分享敏感数据，同时避免报复。
尽管如此，没有什么是十全十美的，联邦调查局过去曾使用过多种技术来追踪Tor的用户。例如，2016年，该机构发现有超过135人访问了一个儿童色情网站。2018年和2019年，联邦调查局能够追踪并逮捕数十名被控贩毒的Tor用户。
美国司法部，特别是司法部长比尔·巴尔(Bill Barr)，也多次对强加密的想法表示敌意。
巴尔在整整一年前的一次演讲中说，免搜查令加密已经给社会带来了巨大的成本，并补充说，加密的通信服务可以而且必须安装后门，以免互联网成为一个无法无天的区域。
note_en:
A representative from the Department of Justice suggested in 2018 that the Federal Bureau of Investigation should have a look into NPR's use of a secure, encrypted tipline, newly publicized emails reveal.
 Reporter Jason Leopold obtained an email exchange from DOJ officials from a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request and  shared them on Twitter. The email thread begins with an April 2018 message from Neil McCabe, who was at the time a reporter at One America News Network (OANN), a far-right cable news channel best known for boosting and spreading conspiracy theories. McCabe was writing to Lauren Ehrsam Gorey, who was then a spokesperson in the DOJ's Office of Public Affairs (i.e., the department's communications and public relations division).
 "Can you find out if DOJ is cool with NPR running a Tor-enabled tip email?" McCabe wrote, adding a link to NPR's  instructions for sending in confidential tips.
 National Public Radio is an independent, nonprofit radio and media company that has about 1,000 "member stations" nationwide, much in the same way a TV network such as ABC or CBS has local affiliate stations in most cities. McCabe's explanation for asking the DOJ about NPR's tipline, however, seems to indicate he believed it to be a state-run outlet:
 In Army public affairs, the reason public affairs soldiers do not break stories about crimes or corruption is that the Army actually has its own police force and detectives, the MPs. That makes it absurd that some reporter at an Army newspaper would be running down crimes—rather, as soon as a public affairs soldier becomes aware of wrongdoing, he is supposed to report it to the authorities.
 How is it then, that federal government employees could be running their own private solo for information about crimes or national security risks?
 Rather than correcting McCabe in any way, however, DOJ representative Ian Prior responded: "You should reach out to FBI for this."
  The "Tor-enabled tip email" in question is SecureDrop, an open source document-submission system managed by the  Freedom of the Press Foundation. NPR  uses SecureDrop to allow for individuals to send in anonymous tips. Most major national and international media outlets, including the Associated Press, The New York Times, The Washington Post, ProPublica, Reuters, and  dozens of others, also use SecureDrop.
 Any given newsroom's SecureDrop portal can only be accessed through the  Tor browser—anonymity-minded software that actively works to block the kind of tracking that makes most Web use easily identifiable.
 Because Tor makes it harder to identify users, it's often used as the gateway to the  Dark Web, that out-of-view corner of the Internet where crime and criminals tend to congregate. But there are plenty of valid, legal reasons why someone would want to screen their own identity—including, but not limited to, sharing sensitive data with investigative journalists while avoiding retaliation.
 Still, nothing's perfect, and the FBI has used a  variety of techniques to track Tor users in the past. In 2016, for example, the agency identified  more than 135 individuals accessing a child pornography site. In  2018 and  2019, the FBI was able to track and arrest dozens of Tor users on drug-trafficking charges.
 The DOJ generally, and Attorney General Bill Barr specifically, have also  repeatedly  expressed  hostility to the very idea of strong encryption.
 Barr said  in a speech exactly one year ago that "warrant-proof encryption is already imposing huge costs on society," adding that encrypted communications services "can and must" have backdoors put in, lest the Internet become "a law-free zone."
title: 电子邮件显示，美国司法部建议OANN就NPR的回线打电话给FBI
title_en: DOJ suggested OANN should call FBI about NPR’s tipline, emails show

note:
本周早些时候，堪萨斯州托皮卡的一个学区宣布，作为重新开学计划的一部分，它将在员工体温检查亭使用面部识别技术。不幸的是，这样的系统将不能防止病毒的无症状传播-这是新冠肺炎最具挑战性的特征之一。
由于这场流行病仍在肆虐美国，学校重新开学的问题已经变得非常政治化。在本月早些时候的一次简报会上，白宫新闻秘书凯利·麦克纳尼(Kayleigh McEnany)辩称，“科学不应该阻碍”重新开放。
“面部识别公司会从任何角度向学校推销他们的产品--但这一次绝对是荒谬的，”争取未来运动主任凯特琳·西利·乔治(Caitlin Seeley George)在谈到这项技术在堪萨斯州的学校实施计划时说。“面部识别阻止不了新冠肺炎的传播，学校不应该相信这个骗局。”
纽约的禁令被数字隐私倡导者视为一次重大胜利，他们不仅质疑这项监控技术对公民自由的潜在担忧，还质疑这项技术实现其宣称目标的能力。这种技术的有效性在显示高假阳性率和编码到系统本身的种族偏见的研究中一再受到抨击。
纽约大学教育政策中心副主任斯蒂芬妮·科伊尔(Stefan Ie Coyle)说：“多年来，我们一直在说，面部识别和其他生物识别监控技术在学校是没有立足之地的，这是保护学生免受这种侵入性监控的巨大飞跃。”
“学校应该是一个孩子们可以学习和成长的环境，而一个有缺陷和种族歧视的系统的存在，不断地监控学生，这使得这是不可能的。”
note_en:
Earlier this week, a  school district in Topeka, Kansas announced that it would employ facial recognition technology at a temperature check kiosk for staff as part of its plan to reopen schools. Unfortunately, such a system would not be capable of preventing asymptomatic spread of the virus—one of COVID-19’s most challenging features.
 With the pandemic still ravaging the U.S., the issue of school reopening has become deeply politicized. In a briefing earlier this month, White House press secretary  Kayleigh McEnany argued that “science should not stand in the way” of reopenings.
  “Facial recognition companies will use any angle they can to market their product to schools—but this one is just absolutely ridiculous,”  Fight for the Future Campaign Director Caitlin Seeley George said of the technology’s proposed school implementation in Kansas. “Facial recognition will not stop the spread of COVID-19, and schools shouldn’t buy into this hokum.”
 New York’s moratorium was viewed as a major victory by digital privacy advocates, who call into question not only the surveillance technology’s potential  concerns for civil liberties but also the tech’s ability to accomplish its stated goals at all. The efficacy of such technology has come under fire repeatedly in studies demonstrating  high false positive rates and racial biases coded into the systems themselves.
 “We’ve said for years that facial recognition and other biometric surveillance technologies have no place in schools, and this is a monumental leap forward to protect students from this kind of invasive surveillance,” NYCLU Education Policy Center Deputy Director Stefanie Coyle  said.
 “Schools should be an environment where children can learn and grow, and the presence of a flawed and racially-biased system constantly monitoring students makes that impossible.”
title: 纽约立法机构投票决定在两年内停止学校使用面部识别技术
title_en: New York legislature votes to halt facial recognition tech in schools for two years

note:
佐治亚理工学院研究员马克·里德尔(Mark Riedl)没有想到，他的机器学习模型“怪异的A.I.Yankovic”会给现有歌曲生成新的押韵歌词，会带来任何麻烦。但它确实发生了。
5月15日，Reidl发布了一段人工智能生成的抒情视频，视频中有迈克尔·杰克逊(Michael Jackson)“Beat It”的器乐演奏者。Reidl在推特上写道，在Twitter收到代表主要和独立唱片公司的国际唱片业联合会(International Federation Of The Phonography Industry)发布的数字千年版权法版权侵权通知后，该网站于7月14日被删除。
“我相当确信我的视频得到了合理的使用，”Riedl在谈到他的人工智能创作时告诉Motherboard，他的创作显然是受到了奇怪的Al&39；的恶搞的启发。Riedl说，他在Twitter上发布的其他人工智能生成的抒情视频还没有被删除。
Riedl与Twitter对撤下提出了异议，但尚未收到回应。推特也没有回应母板的置评请求。
这一事件提出了一个问题，即机器学习在已经存在细微差别和复杂的合理使用规则方面发挥了什么作用，这些规则允许在某些情况下使用受版权保护的作品，包括教育用途，并将其作为“变革性”作品的一部分。在某些情况下，合理使用也可以保护恶搞。
Riedl的研究重点是人工智能和娱乐故事的研究，他说这个模型是作为一个个人项目创建的，超出了他在佐治亚理工学院的角色。“怪异的A.I.Yankovic生成了另一种歌词，与现有歌曲的押韵和音节方案相匹配。然后，这些不同的歌词就可以用原来的曲调演唱了。“Riedl说。选择韵律，然后使用两个神经网络GPT-2和XLNET逐字生成每行。
值得一提的是，据“纽约时报”报道，怪人阿尔·扬科维奇在恶搞一首歌之前，需要征得艺术家的同意。看起来，加入乐器“击败它”是引发IFPI下架通知的原因，但Riedl仍然相信他的视频受到合理使用的保护。
里德尔说：“我会争辩说，我的系统正在生成恶搞歌词，我不需要版权所有者的许可就可以发布恶搞内容。”“不过，我不是律师。”
在确定作品是否受到合理使用保护时，考虑的标准包括受版权保护的作品的性质、所使用的受版权保护的材料的数量和质量，以及新的和未经许可的作品所创作的原创作品对市场的影响。另一个重要的标准是使用的目的和特征，包括新作品是否具有任何形式的变革性。在一个充斥着粉丝制作的剪辑超级剪辑的世界里，这一点尤其相关，这些剪辑设置为音乐、模因、视频恶搞和其他类型的在线内容，这些内容往往采用受版权保护的材料，并将其转换为不同的东西。
科罗拉多博尔德大学(University Of Colorado Boulder)信息科学教授凯西·菲斯勒(Casey Fiesler)在变革工作组织(Organization For Transformative Works)的法律委员会工作。她说，里德尔的视频与超级剪辑的视频相似，因为它有视觉上的转换，但音频没有转换，她说这会让合理使用的论点变得更困难。然而，菲斯勒说，在她看来，里德尔的视频应该被认为是合理使用的一个例子。
“这款新产品既是恶搞，又是一种卡拉OK风格的视频。这绝对不同于原版歌曲，这也是变革性的一种含义。“菲斯勒说。
但菲斯勒说，无论哪种方式，都可以提出论点，根据她的工作性质，她是更广泛地适用合理使用法的倡导者。菲斯勒说：“合理使用是这样一种事情，如果你问像我这样的人，你会得到一个与问娱乐公司内部法律顾问截然不同的答案。”
Riedl视频的下架引发了另一个有趣的问题：在合理使用保护的背景下，一部变革性的作品是由算法而不是人创造的，这是否重要。
菲斯勒说，她不这么认为，并指出了一个法庭案件，在该案件中，谷歌与主要研究图书馆合作，将谷歌图书的整个藏书数字化，被认为是受到合理使用的保护。
菲斯勒说：“例如，在谷歌图书中，案件稍微转向了这样一个事实，即谷歌不仅仅是抄袭这本书，它还提供了关于这本书的信息和统计数据。”“这一切都是由一个算法完成的。”
Fiesler说，重要的是要考虑是否使用了算法来发送拆除通知，平台通常会自动采取行动，以避免诉讼。菲斯勒过去曾写道，依赖算法而不是人类版主可能会给错误留下更大的空间，并导致非侵权作品的删除-特别是那些具有变革性和受合理使用保护的作品。
当被要求置评时，IFPI不愿透露主板如何确定这一具体的下线通知的细节，但一位发言人确实表示，这是自动和手动流程的结合。
“虽然我们没有给出具体内容保护工作的明确细节，但我们使用手动和自动程序相结合的方式。作为协议的一部分，这个例子将被手动检查合理使用声明，“IFPI发言人在一封电子邮件中写道。
归根结底，奇怪的A.I.Yankovic是里德尔为了好玩而创建的个人项目，但围绕他的一段视频被撤下的讨论谈到了版权法和人工智能这个更大的问题，以及法律是否会随着快速变化的技术而演变。
签署“色情通讯”，即表示您同意接收来自“色情通讯”的电子通讯，其中有时可能包括广告或赞助内容。
note_en:
Georgia Tech researcher Mark Riedl didn’t expect that his machine learning model “Weird A.I. Yankovic,” which generates new rhyming lyrics for existing songs would cause any trouble. But it did.
  On May 15, Reidl posted an AI-generated lyric video featuring the instrumental to Michael Jackson’s “Beat It.” It was taken down on July 14, Reidl tweeted, after Twitter received a Digital Millennium Copyright Act takedown notice for copyright infringement from the International Federation of the Phonographic Industry, which represents major and independent record companies.
   “I am fairly convinced that my videos fall under fair use,” Riedl told Motherboard of his AI creation, which is obviously inspired by Weird Al's parodies. Riedl said his  other AI-generated lyric videos posted to Twitter have not been taken down.
  Riedl has contested the takedown with Twitter but has not received a response. Twitter also did not respond to Motherboard’s request for comment.
  The incident raises the question of what role machine learning plays when it comes to the already nuanced and complicated rules of fair use, which allows for the  use of a copyrighted work in certain circumstances, including educational uses and as part of a “transformative” work. Fair use also protects parody in some circumstances.
  Riedl, whose research focuses on the  study of artificial intelligence and storytelling for entertainment, says the model was created as a personal project and outside his role at Georgia Tech. “Weird A.I. Yankovic generates alternative lyrics that match the rhyme and syllables schemes of existing songs. These alternative lyrics can then be sung to the original tune,” Riedl said. “Rhymes are chosen, and two neural networks, GPT-2 and XLNET, are then used to generate each line, word by word.”
  It’s worth noting, according to the  New York Times  Weird Al Yankovic asks for permission from an artist before he parodies a song. It appears that the inclusion of the instrumental to “Beat It” is what triggered the takedown notice by IFPI, but Riedl is still convinced his videos are protected under fair use.
   “I would argue that my system is generating parody lyrics and that I do not require permission from the copyright holder to publish parody content,” Riedl said. “I am not a lawyer, however.”
  The criteria considered when determining if a work is protected under fair use include the nature of the copyrighted work, the quantity and quality of the copyrighted material used, and the effect on the market of the original work created by the new and unlicenced work. Another important criteria is the purpose and character of the use, including whether the new work is in any way transformative. This is particularly pertinent in a world full of fan-made supercuts of clips set to music, memes, video parodies, and other types of online content that often take copyrighted material and transform it to something different.
  Casey Fiesler, a professor of Information Science at the University of Colorado Boulder who works on the legal committee for the  Organization for Transformative Works, said Riedl’s video is similar to supercut videos in that there is a visual transformation but the audio isn't transformed, which she said can make for a harder fair use argument. However, Fiesler said in her opinion that Riedl’s video should be considered an example of fair use.
  “This new product is both a parody and a sort of karaoke-style video. It's definitely really different than what the original song is, which is kind of what transformativeness is,” Fiesler said.
   But Fiesler said arguments can always be made either way, and by the nature of her work she is an advocate for a more expansive application of fair use laws. “Fair use is one of those things where, if you ask someone like me, you'll get a very different answer than if you asked the in-house counsel of an entertainment company,” Fiesler said.
  The takedown of Riedl’s video raises another interesting question: whether it matters that a transformative work is created by an algorithm and not a human when considered in the context of fair use protection.
  Fiesler said she doesn’t think it does, and pointed to  a court case in which Google’s collaboration with major research libraries to digitize entire book collections for Google Books was deemed protected under fair use.
  “So in Google Books, for example, the case turned slightly on the fact that Google wasn't just copying the book, it was giving information about the book and providing stats,” Fiesler said. “That was an algorithm doing all that.”
  Fiesler said it is important to consider whether an algorithm was used to send the takedown notice, which are often automatically acted upon by platforms to avoid lawsuits. Fiesler has written in the past that  relying on algorithms and not human moderators can leave more room for error and result in the removal of non-infringing works—particularly those that are transformative and protected under fair use.
  When asked for comment, IFPI would not give Motherboard details on how this specific takedown notice was determined, but a spokesperson did say it was a combination of automated and manual processes.
  “Whilst we don’t give explicit details of our specific content protection work, we use a combination of manual and automatic processes. This example would have been manually checked for a fair use claim as part of the protocol,” the IFPI spokesperson wrote in an email.
  Ultimately, Weird A.I. Yankovic is a personal project that Riedl created for fun, but the discussion around the takedown of one of his videos speaks to the larger issue of copyright law and AI, and whether the laws will evolve along with the rapidly changing technology.
  By signing up to the VICE newsletter you agree to receive electronic communications from VICE that may sometimes include advertisements or sponsored content.
title: 唱片业正在追逐由算法创作的恶搞歌曲
title_en: The Record Industry Is Going After Parody Songs Written By an Algorithm
