410万这一数字被细分为大约300万零售用户和超过100万批发用户，他们通过AT&amp；T无线套餐之一获得这项新服务，该服务是捆绑在那里的。
尽管HBO Max还为时过早，但这些数字表明，绝大多数传统HBO客户还没有尝试过HBO Max，尽管他们可以免费使用。目前，HBO客户可以使用他们的有线或卫星电视提供商帐户信息向HBO Max进行身份验证。与此同时，HBO Now的订户在Hulu、移动应用程序、部分ISP和HBO Now网站上都会自动升级到Max。
从消费者的角度来看，HBO的战略一直令人困惑。HBO是众所周知的付费频道，主要是成人内容。这款香奈儿曾在移动设备上分发，就像HBO Go针对传统付费电视客户一样，而现在HBO针对的是过度用户。随着HBO Max的推出，目标一直是将HBO转变为类似于Netflix的面向整个家庭的更广泛的服务。为此，HBO、WarnerMedia和其他授权内容被合并到一个屋檐下。
美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)今天表示，与HBO Now相比，HBO Max用户每周平均多花70%的时间观看这项服务。它还强调了其原创内容的受欢迎程度，指出其所有6部新原创电视剧都跻身该平台上观看次数最多的25部电视剧之列。到8月份，HBO Max平台上将有21部新的原创剧集。
但华纳传媒仍然希望向其规模更大的现有客户群分发“标准”HBO，并且除了众多付费电视提供商外，还在Hulu等各种流媒体电视服务和Apple TV等平台上达成了多项协议。此外，HBO在亚马逊和Roku等平台上作为附加付费订阅出售。
这使得消费者很难理解他们可以获得哪个版本的HBO，以及它将在哪里工作。
华纳媒体(WarnerMedia)尚未能够与美国两大流媒体平台提供商亚马逊(Amazon)和Roku签署HBO Max的协议，这让这一重大挑战变得更加严峻。这两家公司控制着70%的市场份额。这意味着，听说过这项新服务的消费者将无法在这些设备上找到这款应用。
他说：“我们反复尝试让所有使用亚马逊Fire设备的客户都能使用HBO Max，包括那些通过亚马逊购买HBO的客户。”“不幸的是，亚马逊对待HBO Max及其客户的方式，与他们对待其他服务及其客户的方式不同。”
这些评论明显跳过了对Roku的任何提及，就在几天前，亚马逊首席执行官杰夫·贝佐斯将与苹果、谷歌和Facebook的首席执行官一起在众议院司法反垄断委员会作证，这是该委员会正在对数字市场潜在的反竞争行为进行调查的一部分。
该委员会担心的一个领域是科技公司对其数字市场的权力和控制权，它们在数字市场上设定条款，禁止应用和服务的分发，并从与自己竞争的企业收取佣金。
在这种情况下，美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)与亚马逊(Amazon)的问题，与它希望如何在媒体平台上分发HBO Max有关。随着战略的转变，AT&amp；T的目标是为消费者提供一款类似于Netflix的独立应用--就像它现在在苹果电视(Apple TV)和安卓电视(Android TV)上所做的那样。但亚马逊和Roku也希望像目前通过亚马逊Prime Video Channel平台和Roku Channel上的Roku Premium订阅平台销售HBO Max的订阅一样。
随着Roku对Roku Channel的投资，它已经与曾经的中立平台保持了距离，因为它现在的动机是围绕Roku Channel的订阅市场达成有利于自己目标的交易，就像亚马逊(Amazon)等其他非中立的参与者一样。这也不是HBO Max独有的问题。出于类似的原因，NBCU的新流媒体服务孔雀在发布时也未能提供Roku和Fire TV支持。不幸的是，消费者才是最终的输家，因为科技巨头不仅在争夺美元，而且从长远来看，谁将拥有客户关系。
如果没有分销，AT&amp；T的WarnerMedia可能会面临挑战，以实现其HBO Max的目标。
然而，该公司声称，到2025年，它在美国的HBO Max订户仍将达到5000万至5500万。作为这一战略的一部分，WarnerMedia还计划在国际上推出HBO Max，并在明年某个时候提供该服务的低成本、AS支持版本。
note_en:
The 4.1 million figure breaks down as around 3 million retail subscribers and over 1 million were wholesale subscribers who came to the new service via one of AT&T wireless plans, where the service is bundled.
 Though it’s still early days for HBO Max, these numbers indicate that the vast majority of traditional HBO customers have not yet tried HBO Max, even though it’s free for them to use. Currently, HBO customers can authenticate with HBO Max  using their cable or satellite TV provider account information. HBO Now subscribers, meanwhile,  are automatically upgraded to Max across Hulu, mobile apps, select ISPs, and the HBO Now site.
  The HBO strategy, from a consumer perspective, has been confusing. HBO is known as premium channel with mostly adult content. This chanel had been distributed across mobile devices as HBO GO for traditional pay TV customers and HBO Now for over-the-top users. With the launch of HBO Max, the goal has been to transform HBO into a broader offering for the whole family, similar to Netflix. To do so, HBO, WarnerMedia and other licensed content was combined under one roof.
 AT&T said today that HBO Max customers spent, on average, 70% more time viewing the service on a weekly basis, compared with HBO Now. It also stressed the popularity of its original content, noting that all 6 of its new originals found themselves ranked among the top 25 viewed series on the platform. By August, HBO Max will have 21 new original series on the platform.
 But WarnerMedia still wants to distribute “standard” HBO to its larger, existing customer base, and has a number of deals in place to do so across a variety of streaming TV services, like Hulu, and platforms, like Apple TV, in addition to numerous pay TV providers. In addition, HBO is sold as an add-on premium subscription across some platforms, like Amazon and Roku.
 That makes it difficult for consumers to understand which version of HBO they can get and where it will work.
 That significant challenge is made worse by the fact that WarnerMedia has not yet been able to ink deals for HBO Max with the two top streaming media platform providers in the U.S.: Amazon and Roku,  which control 70% of the market. That means consumers who have heard of the new service won’t be able to find the app on these devices.
  “We’ve tried repeatedly to make HBO Max available to all customers using Amazon Fire devices, including those customers that have purchased HBO via Amazon,” he said. “Unfortunately, Amazon has taken an approach of treating HBO Max and its customers differently than how they’ve chosen to treat other services, and their customers.”
 The comments, which notably skip over any mention of Roku, come only days before  Amazon CEO Jeff Bezos is set to testify before the House Judiciary Antitrust Subcommittee, along with CEOs from Apple, Google and Facebook, as part of the Committee’s ongoing investigation of potential anti-competitive practices in the digital marketplace.
 One area of concern for the Committee is the power and control the tech companies have over their digital marketplaces, where they set terms, ban apps and services from distribution, and take commissions from businesses that compete with their own.
 AT&T’s issue with Amazon, in this case, has to do with how it wants to distribute HBO Max across the media platforms. With its shift in strategy, AT&T aims to offer consumers a standalone app, similar to Netflix — as it does now on Apple TV and Android TV. But Amazon and Roku want to also sell subscriptions to HBO Max like they currently do for HBO through the  Amazon Prime Video Channels platform and Roku’s  Premium Subscription platform on The Roku Channel.
 With Roku’s investment in The Roku Channel it’s been  distancing itself from being the neutral platform it once was, as it’s now motivated to make deals that benefit its own goals around The Roku Channel’s subscription marketplace, the same as other non-neutral players, like Amazon. This is not a problem unique to HBO Max, either. NBCU’s new streaming service Peacock also  failed to offer Roku and Fire TV support at launch, for similar reasons. Unfortunately, the consumer is the one who ultimately loses here as tech giants grapple over not only the dollars, but who will own the customer relationship in the long run.
 Without distribution, AT&T’s WarnerMedia could be challenged to meet its goals for HBO Max.
 The company, however, claims it’s still on track for 50-55 million HBO Max subscribers in the U.S by 2025. As part of this strategy, WarnerMedia also plans to launch HBO Max internationally and offer a lower-cost, as-supported version of the service sometime next year.
title: HBO Max在第一个月就达到了410万订户，尽管在Roku和Fire TV上缺乏分销
title_en: HBO Max reached 4.1M subscribers in first month, despite lack of distribution on Roku and Fire TV

根据T-Mobile的内部文件，该运营商计划快速追踪关闭其2G和3G语音网络的计划，受影响的客户可能会在几个月内被迫升级手机或离开网络。大多数运营商已经关闭了他们传统的2G网络，因此这些频率可以重新用于LTE和5G，3G的到期日很快就会到来。美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T；)昨日开始警告客户，从2022年开始，没有4G语音通话的手机将不再起作用。
安卓警方获得的一份T-Mobile内部文件如下所示，从今年8月4日开始-仅几周后-T-Mobile将要求网络上的所有新设备都支持LTE语音(VoLTE)。不兼容手机的现有客户不会立即受到影响，但从2021年1月开始，所有不兼容的设备都将不能再使用T-Mobile，甚至可能完全不能使用。在不久的将来，受影响的客户将收到一条短信，通知他们这一变化。
这一变化也将影响T-Mobile的MVNO，Metro，前身为MetroPCS。目前还不清楚这一举措是否会适用于其他使用T-Mobile网络的MVNO，如薄荷移动(Mint Mobile)、叮当(Ting)和消费者蜂窝(Consumer Ccell)。我们有理由相信Sprint设备也会受到影响，但对该网络上的客户的影响不太清楚-运营商一直阻止没有明确认证的设备，认证过程多年来一直包括VoLTE支持。
虽然T-Mobile没有像AT&amp；T那样允许VoLTE的白名单系统，但事实仍然是，许多手机与T-Mobile的实现不兼容(或者制造商根本没有增加支持)。新的要求将主要影响使用小米或华为等公司精选进口手机的客户，以及较旧的手机。
在美国销售的大多数解锁手机多年来一直支持T-Mobile的VoLTE配置文件-即使像HTC one M9和LG G2这样的旧设备也可以兼容。强迫人们在全球大流行期间购买新手机，而美国一些地区的失业率高达20%仍然不是一个很好的选择，但T-Mobile是否会为受影响的客户提供新设备折扣仍有待观察。
note_en:
According to internal T-Mobile documents, the carrier plans to rapidly fast track plans to shut down its 2G and 3G voice networks, and affected customers could be forced to upgrade handsets or leave the network in a matter months. Most carriers have already shut down their legacy 2G networks, so the frequencies can be re-used for LTE and 5G, and 3G's expiration date is rapidly approaching.  AT&T started warning customers yesterday that phones without 4G voice calling would no longer work starting in 2022.
 An internal T-Mobile document obtained by  Android Police pictured below states that starting August 4th of this year — mere weeks from now — T-Mobile will require all new devices on the network to support Voice over LTE (VoLTE). Existing customers with a non-compatible phone won't be immediately impacted, but starting in January 2021, all incompatible devices will no longer be able to use T-Mobile, possibly at all. Affected customers will receive a text informing them of the change "in the near future."
  The change will also affect T-Mobile's MVNO, Metro, formerly known as MetroPCS. It's not immediately clear if the move will apply to other MVNOs using T-Mobile's network, like Mint Mobile, Ting, and Consumer Cellular. We have reason to believe Sprint devices will also be affected, but the impact for customers on that network is less clear — the carrier has always blocked devices not explicitly certified, and the certification process has included VoLTE support for years.
 While T-Mobile doesn't have a whitelist system for allowing VoLTE, like AT&T does, the fact still remains that many phones are incompatible with T-Mobile's implementation (or the manufacturer simply never added support). The new requirement will mostly affect customers using select imported phones from companies like Xiaomi or Huawei, in addition to older phones.
  Most unlocked phones sold in the United States have supported T-Mobile's VoLTE profile for years — even devices as old as the  HTC One M9 and  LG G2 are compatible. Forcing people to purchase new phones during a global pandemic when unemployment in some areas of the United States is  as high as 20% still isn't a  great look, but it remains to be seen if T-Mobile will offer discounts on new devices for affected customers.
title: 内部文档：T-Mobile要求所有新设备从8月4日起支持VoLTE，并从2021年1月起停止支持其网络上的任何非VoLTE电话
title_en: Internal doc: T-Mobile to require all new devices to support VoLTE from August 4, and stop supporting any non-VoLTE phones on its network from January 2021
